title: lupinus littoralis var. layneae
intro: Lupinus littoralis var. layneae, also known as Layne's lupine, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the lupine family and is native to California in the United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, Lupinus littoralis var. layneae is typically made into a tea or tincture, and it can also be used as a poultice or compress for topical applications.
